extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won from long range after GAVIRIA Fernando went too early from 250m, Phillips closing but falling short by three bike lengths.
tactical_patterns: GAVIRIA Fernando's three-bike-length lead from 250m evaporated when JAKOBSEN Fabio found cleaner center-road line versus edges.
GIRMAY Biniam punctured 10km out yet salvaged fourth, suggesting Intermarché sprint train dysfunction rather than form deficit.
Jayco and Soudal-QuickStep shared pace-making burden before circuit, revealing neither team confident monopolizing finale control.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Opened season slowly but won with longest sprint of contenders, suggesting peak form delayed versus 2022 trajectory.
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Led by three lengths at 250m but faded badly, indicating either positioning error or insufficient top-end speed.
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: Fourth after 10km mechanical demonstrates exceptional chase capability and form despite zero leadout support.
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: Third despite chasing from Groenewegen's wheel suggests leadout malfunction but finishing speed intact.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Working for Jakobsen reveals QuickStep prioritizing sprint stages over GC aggression even with former world champion.
